summary: This sprint plan targets the right 5 findings, but two tasks have acceptance criteria/implementation gaps that will likely cause the work to stall or ship incorrect behavior.

blocking_issues.location: Task 1.1: Fix glob-to-regex conversion in capability matching
blocking_issues.issue: Brace expansion support is required by acceptance criteria but the implementation plan does not define how to implement it, and the proposed conversion order will break some patterns.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: The AC explicitly requires `glob_to_regex "src/lib.{ts,js}" handles braces`, but the implementation only mentions escaping metacharacters + `**/*/?` conversions. Also, escaping `{` and `}` before converting braces makes it impossible to implement brace alternation later; if you don’t implement braces, the task can’t be marked done per AC.
blocking_issues.fix: Either (a) remove brace handling from AC (if out of scope for the finding) or (b) specify and implement brace conversion: convert `{a,b}` -> `(a|b)` (including escaping inner tokens appropriately), and adjust conversion order (handle brace groups before escaping `{}` or exclude `{}` from the initial escape set and escape them after brace processing). Add at least one concrete test case for nested/edge braces or explicitly state limitations (e.g., no nesting).

blocking_issues.location: Task 1.4: Remainder distribution in budget allocator
blocking_issues.issue: Remainder distribution algorithm is underspecified and can fail acceptance criteria when the first capability is at max or when remainder > 1.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: AC requires sum(allocations) == total and no capability exceeds max_tokens. The plan says “Distribute remainder to the first capability … that hasn't hit its max,” but doesn’t define what happens if that capability is already at max, or if remainder is larger than remaining headroom. In those cases, the implementation could either violate max_tokens or leave tokens undistributed, failing AC.
blocking_issues.fix: Define a deterministic loop: while remainder > 0, iterate capabilities in priority order and increment any capability with remaining headroom by 1 until remainder is 0; if no headroom exists anywhere, document that remainder is intentionally dropped (or adjust earlier proportional step to respect global headroom). Add a test/AC example covering: (1) top capability at max, (2) remainder > 1, (3) all at max.
